target.name: H2Pro
target.founder: Gideon Grader, Avner Rothschild, Hen Dotan, Talmon Marco

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
H2Pro is a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $100M+ to date. Valuation assessment draws on a peer set of 6 same-stage, same-sector companies, supplemented by stage-progression analysis. The comparable universe includes both private peers (Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B, Pano AI at $500M, Sage Geosystems at $400M, Reverion at $310M, and CarbonCapture Inc. at $300M) and one public benchmark.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to $100M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $190M. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company Climate & Energy cohort at identical stage yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The peer distribution reflects material heterogeneity in company maturity and technology scope within the sector.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $162M (low) to $408M (high), with a median estimate of $272M. This midpoint sits meaningfully above stage-progression guidance, reflecting peer valuations in the Climate & Energy sector. Key uncertainties center on technology differentiation relative to peers, capital efficiency trajectory, and near-term market validation. The wide peer range ($120M–$5.0B) underscores valuation sensitivity to underlying technical risk and commercialization progress.
